title: Technical Problem Solving, Coding, and Work-Sample Boundaries · Career Campaign Defense
summary: A candidate and hiring group preparing an accessible screen, technical exercise, work-sample decision, and explicit next-step handoff. They need concise evidence, protected private boundaries, accessible process requests, contract-first problem solving, changed-case testing, honest tool use, and proportional work-sample consent. Planned artifact: Deliver Accessible Multi-Stage Interview Loop with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progr…
objectives: Deliver a flexible evidence-grounded introduction connecting target work, relevant behavior, proof, and reason for this conversation.
Request process details and needed access adjustments with learner-controlled disclosure and location-specific rights awareness.
Clarify user, input, output, constraints, examples, error behavior, environment, allowed tools, and evaluation before implementation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Project Concise Introduction
steps.instruction:
project case — A timed task begins without contract or tool rules, optimizes before correctness, tests only the sample, and quietly resembles unpaid production work. The project variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: defend one bounded career decision

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Project Concise Introduction.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for deliver a flexible evidence-grounded introduction connecting target work, relevant behavior, proof, and reason for this conversation. For Commit to the mission model · Project Concise Introduction, keep Concise Introduction connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, apply Sample Boundary without its stated preconditions, omit the observable technical interview practice system trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Sample Boundary for observable technical interview practice system.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Sample Boundary condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Sample Boundary output and hide the violated observable technical interview practice system assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Sample Boundary.

steps.title: Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Sample Boundary
steps.instruction:
Write the project evidence note for Sample Boundary: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A timed task begins without contract or tool rules, optimizes before correctness, tests only the sample, and quietly resembles unpaid production work. The project variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Evaluate take-home scope, ownership, confidentiality, accessibility, tool disclosure, deadline, compensation, and production-use risk before consent.

Evidence target: Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Sample Boundary.
steps.why: Retrieving Sample Boundary through the concrete observable technical interview practice system case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Sample Boundary, keep Sample Boundary connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Evaluate take-home scope, ownership, confidentiality, accessibility, tool disclosure, deadline, compensation, and production-use risk before consent.

hints: For Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Sample Boundary, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Sample Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Sample Boundary, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A timed task begins without contract or tool rules, optimizes before correctness, tests only the sample, and quietly resembles unpaid production work. The project variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Evaluate take-home scope, ownership, confidentiality, accessibility, tool disclosure, deadline, compensation, and production-use risk before consent.

For Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Sample Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: Every interview assignment is reasonable because participation is optional.
